name: cable bench press
instructions: Adjust the cable machine to chest height and attach the handles.
Stand facing away from the machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Grasp the handles with an overhand grip and step forward to create tension in the cables.
Position your feet firmly on the ground and engage your core.
Bend your elbows and bring your hands to shoulder level, keeping your elbows at a 90-degree angle.
Push the handles forward, extending your arms fully in front of you.
Pause for a moment, then slowly reverse the movement, bringing your hands back to shoulder level.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

Adjust the cable machine to chest height and attach the handles. Stand facing away from the machine with your feet shoulder-width apart. Grasp the handles with an overhand grip and step forward to create tension in the cables. Position your feet firmly on the ground and engage your core. Bend your elbows and bring your hands to shoulder level, keeping your elbows at a 90-degree angle. Push the handles forward, extending your arms fully in front of you. Pause for a moment, then slowly reverse the movement, bringing your hands back to shoulder level. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

conditionNotes.note: Keep load symmetrical and stop at positions that create sharp pain or twisting.
Use pain-free range of motion, slower tempo, and lighter progressions when joints feel irritated.
Weight-bearing resistance exercises are beneficial for bone density when load is appropriate.
Maintain neutral spine and avoid positions that aggravate the lower back.
Maintain consistent breathing throughout. Avoid breath-holding.
Both aerobic and resistance training improve insulin sensitivity. Monitor blood sugar before, during, and after sessions. Have a fast-acting carb available.
Rhythm-based exercises and large-amplitude movements are beneficial. Focus on motor confidence.
Focus on muscle co-contraction and joint stability. Avoid locking out joints passively.
Avoid supine positions after the first trimester due to vena cava compression. Incline or side-lie alternatives preferred.
Start light with higher reps and shorter ROM. Progress load gradually as pain-free capacity increases.
